message: br-bd-h09f7 add btrfs rename missing-source ENOENT coverage

 {"id":"bd-gqvss","title":"add mounted-path btrfs symlink target PATH_MAX boundary and ENAMETOOLONG coverage","description":"Survey finding: btrfs mounted-path FUSE E2E covers symlink creation/follow, while ffs-core separately enforces the public PATH_MAX boundary for symlink targets. Scope: add mounted-path btrfs probes that accept a target exactly at PATH_MAX, reject an over-limit target with ENAMETOOLONG, and verify failed creation leaves no orphan entry. Acceptance: fuse_e2e freezes the mounted-path btrfs symlink target-length contract instead of relying only on core-unit coverage.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T09:00:26.164538258Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-21T09:00:26.164538258Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-gto9j","title":"add mounted-path ext4 write-to-directory EISDIR coverage","description":"Survey finding: ext4 mounted-path FUSE E2E covers regular-file write and append behavior, but the operator-visible refusal for writing directly to a directory is only frozen in ffs-core unit tests. Scope: add an ext4 mounted-path probe that opens the root or a created directory for writing, asserts exact EISDIR, and verifies directory entries remain unchanged. Acceptance: fuse_e2e freezes the ext4 write-to-directory public contract.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T09:15:02.912544030Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-22T01:29:14.280124692Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T01:29:14.277587565Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-gwfj","title":"Add btrfs chunk item adversarial parser coverage","description":"Fallback testing-fuzzing parity round: add deterministic adversarial regression coverage for ffs-btrfs chunk-tree item parsing. Cover valid multi-stripe payloads, fixed-header truncation, declared stripe-count payload truncation, zero stripes, zero chunk length, zero stripe length, and multiple RAID profile bit rejection; update conformance/COVERAGE.md.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-18T07:55:05.362372454Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-18T08:05:45.836955732Z","closed_at":"2026-04-18T08:05:45.834545421Z","close_reason":"Completed btrfs chunk item adversarial parser coverage; focused parse_chunk_item tests, workspace check, and workspace clippy passed via rch.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["btrfs","fuzz","testing"]}

Workspace clippy/fmt still stop on unrelated pre-existing ffs-tui lifetime and bench import-order drift.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-h182","title":"Implement ext4 external xattr block allocation in write path","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-19T07:18:31.571096990Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-19T07:33:11.638236456Z","closed_at":"2026-02-19T07:33:11.638218612Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["ext4","xattr"]}
 {"id":"bd-h2gm","title":"Add indirect block pointer write support for e2compr beyond direct range","description":"BACKGROUND: The e2compr compressed write path (write_ext4_compressed_cluster, write_ext4_uncompressed_cluster) uses set_indirect_block_ptr to write block pointers in the inode's i_block array. This function currently rejects logical_block >= 12 because entries 12-14 are the single/double/triple indirect pointers, and overwriting them with data block pointers would corrupt the indirect addressing table.

CURRENT LIMITATION: With the smallest cluster size (4 blocks, shift=2) and 4K block size, only clusters at logical blocks 0-3, 4-7, and 8-11 can be written — files up to 48KB. Cluster shift 3 (8 blocks) limits to 32KB. Larger files cannot be compressed at all.

THE READ PATH ALREADY WORKS: resolve_indirect_block handles single/double/triple indirect lookups for reading. The write side needs the inverse operation.

IMPLEMENTATION PLAN:
1. Add write_indirect_block_ptr(cx, scope, inode, alloc, logical_block, phys) that mirrors resolve_indirect_block but writes:
   - For logical_block < 12: direct write to inode.extent_bytes (existing code).
   - For 12 <= logical_block < 12 + ptrs_per_block: allocate or read the single-indirect block (i_block[12]), write the pointer at the correct offset within it.
   - For double-indirect range: allocate/read i_block[13] -> intermediate block -> write pointer.
   - For triple-indirect range: three-level allocation/read.
2. Intermediate blocks must be allocated on first use (when i_block[12/13/14] is 0).
3. Allocated intermediate blocks must be freed when the last pointer in them becomes 0 (during cluster freeing).
4. Update set_indirect_block_ptr callers to use the new function.
5. Add tests for files that span into the indirect range.

COMPLEXITY: Medium-high. The read path is ~120 lines; the write path will be ~200 lines due to allocation of intermediate blocks. Must correctly handle:
- First write to a new indirect level (allocate the intermediate block).
- Overwrite of existing pointers (read-modify-write the intermediate block).
- Freeing of intermediate blocks when all their pointers become 0.

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- Compressed write works for files up to at least 1MB (requires single-indirect support).
- Compressed read/write roundtrip test for a file spanning into indirect block range.
- set_indirect_block_ptr error at lb >= 12 is removed.
- No block leaks: freed clusters also free their intermediate block pointers if they become empty.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:05:10.618016450Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-25T03:53:06.169916785Z","closed_at":"2026-03-25T03:53:06.169440554Z","close_reason":"All subtasks complete: single-indirect (bd-z15u, commit 18cd3d7) and double/triple indirect (bd-pv9l, commit 06a4288). e2compr can now compress files of any size.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["compression","e2compr","ext4"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-h2gm","depends_on_id":"bd-pv9l","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:10:50.900230917Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-h2gm","depends_on_id":"bd-z15u","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:10:49.395648429Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-h3hsy","title":"expose ext4 quota inode metadata in inspect/info output","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T01:24:31.045408586Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-21T04:03:13.350594421Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T04:03:13.347266292Z","close_reason":"Exposed ext4 quota inode metadata in inspect and info output","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

@@ -163,6 +163,8 @@ targets, with no visible workspace-entry or witness-file drift.
 Mounted-path btrfs rename refusal coverage now also freezes exact `ENOTDIR`
 for rename attempts whose destination parent is a regular file, with no source
 or parent-file drift.
+Mounted-path btrfs rename coverage now also freezes exact `ENOENT` for missing
+source paths, with no visible workspace-entry or witness-file drift.
 Mounted-path ext4 symlink/refusal coverage now also freezes exact `EINVAL` for
 `readlink` on both regular-file and directory non-symlink paths, with no
 directory-entry or file-byte drift.
